title: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List

content.problemStatement:
Given a Binary Tree, convert it to a Linked List where the linked list nodes follow the same order as the pre-order traversal of the binary tree.



Use the right pointer of the Binary Tree as the ‘next’ pointer for the linked list and set the left pointer to null. Do this in place and do not create extra nodes.
constraints: 1 <= number of nodes in the binary tree <= 10^4
content.inputFormat: The input is the root of a binary tree where each node has a unique integer value.
content.outputFormat: The output is the root of the modified binary tree where the right pointer of each node points to the next node in the pre-order traversal and the left pointer is set to null.

code.Python3:
# define tree node structure
class TreeNode:
    # constructor to initialize node
    def __init__(self, x: int):
        # store value
        self.val = x
        # left child reference
        self.left = None
        # right child reference
        self.right = None

# solution class holding state
class Solution:
    # initialize previous node reference
    def __init__(self):
        self.prev = None

    # flatten tree to right-skewed linked list (preorder sequence)
    def flatten(self, root: TreeNode) -> None:
        # return if node is null
        if root is None:
            return
        # flatten right subtree first
        self.flatten(root.right)
        # flatten left subtree next
        self.flatten(root.left)
        # connect current node's right to previously processed node
        root.right = self.prev
        # nullify left pointer
        root.left = None
        # update previous to current
        self.prev = root
